title: Aggregate continuity validation now composes strict relation integrity
summary: The repository-wide validator now sends the complete monolithic-plus-additive record set through the strict relation validator after record-shape and reference checks pass. The composed gate therefore includes duplicate relation-target rejection and supersession-cycle rejection. Exact source inspection also exposed a pre-existing schema drift: CM-1000 stores natural-language prose inside its contradicts array rather than record IDs, so the current corpus is expected to remain invalid until that legacy relation is migrated through an explicit preservation-safe correction.

status_relations.observed: The aggregate validator imports validateContinuityRelations and returns its relation summary only when preliminary validation succeeds; adversarial tests now cover duplicate targets and supersession cycles.
status_relations.inferred: The authoritative gate will expose relation-shape drift that was previously latent in additive continuity records.
status_relations.proposed: Create a preservation-safe migration record for CM-1000's prose relation, retain its original blob SHA as historical provenance, and make all machine relations resolve only to CM record IDs.
status_relations.superseded: The prior state in which strict relation integrity existed as a separate optional validator rather than part of the aggregate gate.
status_relations.unresolved: No completed CI execution at the integration commit has been observed, and the current corpus has not yet been migrated to satisfy the stricter relation contract.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the three source blobs. Falsify the convergence claim by showing that the aggregate validator does not invoke the strict relation validator over every loaded record, or that duplicate-target and cycle fixtures pass. Falsify the schema-drift claim by showing that CM-1000.contradicts contains only valid existing CM record IDs. Structural validity does not establish truth of indexed claims.
